commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop + bbox prefilter (#215)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop

Replace the single IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector::Perform call (33.5%
of total instructions in the B4c callgrind profile, 5,591M instructions)
with a custom per-face loop over IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

Two sources of overhead are eliminated:

1. NCollection_Sequence heap management (~31% of total): the ShapeIntersector
   accumulates results in a heap-allocated NCollection_Sequence (ClearSeq,
   Find, AppendPoint, malloc/free).  The per-face loop instead tracks the
   closest hit in a stack-allocated scalar.

2. All-face intersection without prefiltering (~2%): per-face intersectors
   are gated behind Bnd_Box::IsOut(gp_Lin), rejecting faces whose AABB the
   ray misses in O(1).

Implementation details:

- IntersectorCache now holds std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IntCurvesFace_Intersector>>
  (one per face, heap-allocated to avoid movability requirements) and
  std::vector<Bnd_Box> expandedBoxes (each face box enlarged by
  IntersectionTolerance() to prevent IsOut false positives for zero-thickness
  planar face AABBs).

- GetOrCreateIntersector() rebuilds both vectors on generation mismatch.

- DistanceToOut no longer needs the fFaceAdaptorIndex hash-map lookup:
  the loop tracks minFaceIdx directly, giving O(1) access to the cached
  BRepAdaptor_Surface for normal computation.

- DistanceToOut normal condition updated to accept TopAbs_IN || TopAbs_ON,
  fixing pre-existing failures for sphere/cylinder seam hit points that
  IntCurvesFace_Intersector correctly classifies as ON (not IN).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

commit.message:
Smart mouse mode: defer disable until click above viewport (#328)

In smart mode, mouse capture was disabled whenever the mouse moved above
the viewport — too aggressive, preventing hover/movement above the
prompt without losing capture. The intended trigger is user intent to
interact with terminal content above (e.g. text selection), which only a
click signals.

## Changes

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: In the smart mode `on_mouse` handler, gate the
`disable()` call on `MouseEventKind::Down(_)`. Events above the viewport
still return `false` (skipped by flyline) but no longer tear down
capture on mere movement.

```rust
if mouse.row < self.last_viewport_top {
    // Only disable on an actual click above the viewport
    if matches!(mouse.kind, MouseEventKind::Down(_)) {
        self.mouse_state.disable("smart mode: click above the viewport");
    }
    self.last_mouse_over_cell = None;
    return false;
}
```

- **`src/settings.rs`** / **`README.md`**: Updated `MouseMode::Smart`
doc and both README occurrences — "mouse moves above the viewport" →
"user clicks above the viewport".

Auto-update `create-anim` help block in README via workflow (#327)

The `update-settings-docs.yml` workflow only updated the main `flyline
--help` block in the README; the `<!--
FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END -->` block was left stale.

## Changes

- **`docker/flyline_help.Dockerfile`**: Added a `RUN` step to capture
`flyline create-anim --help` (ANSI-stripped) into
`/flyline_create_anim_help.txt`; exports it from the `scratch` stage
alongside `flyline_help.txt`.

- **`.github/workflows/update-settings-docs.yml`**: Extended the Python
README-update script to also read `flyline_create_anim_help.txt` and
replace the `FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START/END` markers:

```python
create_anim_help_text = pathlib.Path("flyline_create_anim_help.txt").read_text().rstrip("\n")
new_create_anim_block = f"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->\n```\n{create_anim_help_text}\n```\n<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->"
updated = re.sub(
    r"<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_START -->.*?<!-- FLYLINE_CREATE_ANIM_HELP_END -->",
    new_create_anim_block,
    updated,
    flags=re.DOTALL,
)
```

Fix auto-save restore: push content in OnAfterRenderAsync after editors exist

OnInitializedAsync runs before the component has rendered, so the Monaco
editor refs don't exist yet. Move the restored content push to
OnAfterRenderAsync (not gated on firstRender), checking hasRestoredState
and schemaEditor is not null. This ensures editors are ready before we
call SetValue on them.

G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Windows smoke crop verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Pivot CI and release flow to Windows only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix auto-save restore: push content into editors from OnInitializedAsync

The previous fix put the editor sync in OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender),
but firstRender fires BEFORE RestoreAutoSavedState completes (the first
await yields to the renderer). By the time the restore sets
hasRestoredState=true, OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender=true) has already
run and won't fire again.

Fix: move the editor push into OnInitializedAsync, right after
RestoreAutoSavedState returns. At that point the editors exist (the
component rendered during the await) so SetValue works. Also guard
OnSchemaEditorInit to skip the model-overwrite when a restore is pending.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Convert all NPC default dialogues to 5-item lists; rewrite 카르니스/루바토/라파엘

- Convert every single-string 'default' value to a Python list of 5 varied responses
- Applies to: 다몬, 오멜룸, 몰, 아라벨라, 제블로어, 브룩샤, 실렌, 알피라, 엘레라신, 게일의 환영, 카엘릭
- Convert all tuple-concatenated multi-line strings (그림자 등불, 파편 수리, etc.) to 5-item lists
- Full rewrite: 카르니스 — cold, contemptuous drider tone (반말); 루바토 — cheerful bard (반말)
- 라파엘 — expand all 5 keywords to 5-item lists in theatrical cambion voice
- Remove 루바토's '타브' keyword entirely per spec
- Keep all 마을/날씨/소문 lists (already 5+) and all affinity tier entries unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Gate mac hidden rendering for CI

Log native runtime initialization failures and add a macOS-only render override that is enabled only during the CI smoke job via MPT_CI_KEEP_RENDERING=1. This keeps the production macOS behavior unchanged while allowing the headless macOS OBS smoke run to keep producing async frames for screenshot validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Gate mac visual smoke on control probe

Add a Darwin-only control-scene probe using OBS's built-in image source so the smoke test only skips visual verification when the runner cannot produce nonblank screenshots even for a known-good built-in scene. Keep the new scene-collection save checkpoint flow and include source activity state in the artifacts for follow-up diagnostics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

Work in squared-distance space throughout PointToMeshDistance traversal
and take a single std::sqrt() in BVHLowerBoundDistance() after traversal
completes. This avoids O(log T + k) sqrt calls per safety query."

commit.message:
Stabilize smoke save and screenshot fallback

Force scene-collection saves by switching through a checkpoint collection during the smoke create phase, then re-validate reloaded settings before restart. Also request fixed-size screenshots and fall back from source captures to scene captures so headless macOS runs can still validate rendered output. Tighten reopen checks so missing scene collections or scenes fail explicitly instead of being recreated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Retry screenshots and persist source settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Tighten mac screenshot and linux quit path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix boot progress to track full download completion, not just headers

fetch() resolves when response headers arrive, but the body may still
be streaming. Now reads the full arrayBuffer before incrementing the
progress counter, then wraps it in a new Response for Blazor to consume.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Heredoc denim quoting</issue_title>
> <issue_description>I don't currently handle heredoc delim quoting.
From the bash reference:
> 
> 
> The format of here-documents is:
> 
> [n]<<[−]word
>         here-document
> delimiter
> The shell does not perform parameter and variable expansion, command
substitution, arithmetic expansion, or filename expansion on word.
> 
> If any part of word is quoted, the delimiter is the result of quote
removal on word, and the lines in the here-document are not expanded. If
word is unquoted, delimiter is word itself, and the here-document text
is treated similarly to a double-quoted string: all lines of the
here-document are subjected to parameter expansion, command
substitution, and arithmetic expansion, the character sequence \newline
is treated literally, and ‘\’ must be used to quote the characters ‘\’,
‘$’, and ‘`’; however, double quote characters have no special meaning.
> 
> 
> Write some test cases in dparser that use a quoted heredoc. Single
quotes, double quotes, and backlashes , and combinations. Add some
simple examples to the command_acceptance module.
> 
> Then see if the contents is appropriately parsed. And try and improve
the parser where required.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

commit.message:
Fix GitHub Pages 404s for reports and missing callgrind links in bench report (#207)

* Initial plan

* Fix GitHub Pages 404s and missing callgrind links in bench report

Three issues fixed in ci.yml:

1. Download ci-reports to docs/ (not docs/reports/) in deploy job.
   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/29cfa731-76d4-4fcc-9fde-1e231f4f8941

commit.message:
Fix sample dropdown: guard against DetachFromSample during programmatic loads, format XML docs in tooltips

Two fixes:
1. Sample selection immediately reset to 'Custom project' because setting
   editor content fired OnSchemaContentChanged -> DetachFromSample. Added
   isLoadingContent guard flag set during sample loads, new project, open
   project, and auto-save restore.
2. 'New' button showed 'Data Object' because activeSampleId was set to a
   non-existent option value. Now uses null consistently for non-sample
   states, matching the '-- Custom project --' placeholder.
3. Signature help tooltips showed raw XML documentation. Added XML doc
   parser that extracts summary, returns, remarks, and per-parameter docs
   into clean Markdown text. JS side passes docs as MarkdownString for
   proper rendering.

commit.message:
Add Task.Yield() before heavy sync work to improve UI responsiveness

In single-threaded Blazor WASM, synchronous Roslyn compilation and code
generation block the UI thread entirely. Add Task.Yield() calls before
heavy work in GenerateAsync, RunAsync, live diagnostics, and
CompilationService.CompileAsync so the browser can render status updates
(spinner, progress text) before the thread is blocked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Cattle Health Monitoring System

- C_RESTAPI: FastAPI backend with MongoDB Atlas
  - JWT + API key authentication
  - Cattle CRUD, sensor data ingestion (bulk)
  - Health evaluation with threshold-based alerts
  - Email notifications with 48h health graphs
  - Role-based access (super_admin/admin/user)

- C_FRONTEND: Streamlit dashboard
  - MongoDB-inspired dark/light theme (default: light)
  - Role-based dashboards (Super Admin/Admin/User)
  - Multi-language support (English/Tamil/Hindi)
  - Real-time cattle health graphs (Plotly)
  - Cattle management, user management, alerts
  - Integrated logo and professional UI

- .gitignore configured (venv, .env, __pycache__, IDE files)
- .env.example files for both frontend and backend

Merge pull request #10 from dchu3/feat/setup-and-configure

fix: address security findings from code review

- setup.sh: replace eval with printf -v to prevent command injection
- setup.sh: chmod 600 .env after writing (owner-only permissions)
- configure.ts: write .env with mode 0o600 to preserve permissions
- configure.ts: suppress echo for secret field input
- configure.ts: validate REMOTE_MCP_URL, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, VERBOSE
  before saving to prevent invalid config that blocks startup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address PR #10 comments and model review findings

PR comments:
- Fix misleading comment in findEnvPath (package.json, not .env)
- Save VERBOSE value even when false/0 (not just true/1)
- Mark TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN prompt as secret to hide input
- Validate REMOTE_MCP_URL protocol in setup.sh (https or localhost http)
- Validate TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID as non-zero integer in setup.sh
- Validate VERBOSE input as true/false/1/0 in setup.sh

GPT 5.4 findings:
- Warn when TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN set without TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
- Quote .env values containing # to prevent dotenv comment truncation

Sonnet 4.6 findings:
- Atomic .env write via temp file + rename (prevents data loss)
- Always chmod 600 .env when present (not just after reconfiguration)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address second round of PR #10 review comments

- Wrap chmodSync in try/catch for Windows/non-POSIX compatibility
- Use 'read -r -s' (separated flags) for consistent backslash handling
- Wrap /configure in try/catch so errors don't crash the CLI
- Validate required fields are present before saving .env

AI-powered spec-driven development framework with 43 specialized skills,
state management, and Azure deployment.

- Monolithic orchestrator (Ralph Loop) + agentskills.io skills
- Greenfield: PRD → FRD → UI → Tests → Contracts → Implementation → Azure
- Brownfield: Extract → Spec-Enable → Assess → Plan → Deliver
- npx aigentik init for easy project setup
- Full documentation suite
- Human gates at every critical transition

commit.message:
Playground: prefixItems, build UX, live diagnostics, signature help

- Show prefixItems in type map for non-pure-tuple arrays (ExplicitTupleType/ImplicitTupleType)
- New Project as separate toolbar button; default to first example on load
- Build UX: console progress lines, CSS spinner in status bar, non-blocking Generate/Run
- Live Roslyn diagnostics: 800ms debounced compilation with Monaco markers as user types
- Signature help: Roslyn semantic model method/constructor parameter info via Monaco provider
- Add IDisposable to MainLayout for timer cleanup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: note clasp run oauth prerequisites

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add clasp remote setup helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address security findings from code review

- setup.sh: replace eval with printf -v to prevent command injection
- setup.sh: chmod 600 .env after writing (owner-only permissions)
- configure.ts: write .env with mode 0o600 to preserve permissions
- configure.ts: suppress echo for secret field input
- configure.ts: validate REMOTE_MCP_URL, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, VERBOSE
  before saving to prevent invalid config that blocks startup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: debug_logger None クラッシュ修正 & Syoboi優先処理順序

[LOG: START] Step 1: バグ修正
- debug_logger が None のとき .debug() を直接呼んでいたクラッシュを
  'if debug_logger:' ガードで修正（syoboi_data_fetched ログ出力箇所）

[LOG: START] Step 2: Grok枠の優先割当
- Step 3 のループ前に prioritized_animes を構築:
  syoboi_hit_animes（Syoboiヒット作品）を先頭に、
  non_syoboi_animes（配信限定・TID未設定）を後尾に配置。
- enumerate(active_animes) → enumerate(prioritized_animes) に変更。
- ループ内部のロジックは無変更。並び順のみ変更で目的達成。

[THOUGHT: 配信限定作品などに Grok 枠を先食いされないよう、
番組表掲載作品（あらすじ未取得の可能性が高い）を優先処理する]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(daily): syoboi_tid設定済み作品はGrok呼出をスキップ

- Syoboi番組表にヒットしなかった作品の処理を二分岐に変更:
  - syoboi_tid が設定されている場合: TV放送作品で今週は放送前/終了/休止と判断し
    Grokを呼び出さず logging.info でスキップ理由を明記
  - syoboi_tid が未設定の場合: 配信限定またはTID未特定の作品として
    従来通り1日5回の枠内でGrokへ問い合わせ
- ログメッセージで判断理由（syoboi_tid値・スキップ理由）が明確に分かるよう調整

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add one-step installer (setup.sh) and /configure CLI command

- setup.sh: curl-pipeable installer that clones to ~/soltrader/,
  installs deps, interactively configures .env, optionally sets up
  dex-trader-mcp, and builds the project
- /configure: new CLI command to view/edit all .env settings at runtime
  with masked secrets, live VERBOSE toggle, and restart notices
- README: add Quick Start section with curl one-liner right after warning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix legacy websocket smoke config

Write OBS-compatible global.ini entries without spaces around '=' so legacy obs-websocket builds read the seeded settings during Linux and macOS smoke runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Monaco race condition: delay Blazor start until Monaco loaded

BlazorMonaco silently fails (console error, no retry) if the monaco
global isn't available when it tries to create editors. On hard refresh,
the AMD require for vs/editor/editor.main may not complete before
Blazor renders the StandaloneCodeEditor components.

Fix: use autostart=false on blazor.webassembly.js and gate Blazor.start()
on a monacoReady promise that resolves when the AMD require completes.
The require() call is issued before jsInterop.js loads (which clobbers
the AMD require with a plain config object).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(season_adder): syoboi詳細取得によるshort_name/official_url優先ロジック追加

- _normalize_for_short_name(): 英語タイトルをCamelCase化するヘルパーを追加
- fetch_syoboi_details(): しょぼいカレンダー個別TID詳細取得関数を追加
  - XMLのCommentフィールドをparseしてx_handle / official_urlを抽出
  - API呼び出し後にtime.sleep(0.5)でサーバー負荷軽減
  - _SYOBOI_DETAIL_URL 定数を追加
- メインループの処理順を最適化:
  1. jikan抽出をsyoboi_tid取得の前に移動（title_english参照のため）
  2. syoboi_tidが存在する場合のみfetch_syoboi_detailsを呼び出す
- short_name決定ロジック（3段階優先）:
  1. syoboi_details["x_handle"] → アンダースコア保持・小文字化
  2. jikan["title_english"] → CamelCase正規化
  3. str(mal_id) → フォールバック
- anime_id生成をgenerate_anime_id()からf-string直接構築に変更
  （アンダースコア保持・ハッシュフォールバック維持）
- official_url決定: syoboi_details優先 → Jikan URLへフォールバック

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Make tuple/array items interactive, show under prefixItems/items headings

- TupleItem: add SchemaPointer and SourceSchemaName for navigation
- Tuple items now clickable: highlights related type, navigates to schema
- Shown under 'prefixItems' heading (matching JSON Schema terminology)
- ArrayItemType: replace flat name strings with TypeMapArrayItemType record
  including SchemaPointer and SourceSchemaName
- Array items shown under 'items' heading as clickable sub-node
- Both highlight related types in the tree when selected
